evidence: drsam-99657.bubble — CustomEvent bTIAw actions bTIBH / bTICE / bTICC
drsam-99657.bubble — APIEvent bTIBO ChangeThing bTICJ on pressure_profile_text

functional_audit.check: resolvePressureProfile implements bTIAw three-branch logic with Bubble high_intensity_text labels and nervous-system display strings
calculateUserPressureProfile reads AUTH-05 loadSignals/stateSignals slugs and patches pressure_profile_text plus results.pressure_profile
runOnboardingProfilePipeline invokes calculateUserPressureProfile immediately after calculateUserClassification

critiques.finding: When two or more high-intensity load signals are present, join order follows LOAD_SIGNAL_QUESTIONS field order (cognitive → relational → environmental → financial) instead of bTIBO load_signal_list construction order (cognitive → environmental → financial → relational). Single- and paired-high cases that include cognitive still match; e.g. all-four-high yields a different permutation than Bubble format_as_text.
critiques.alternative: Collect high-intensity texts in bTIBO list order by iterating extractLoadSignalSlugs output (which mirrors the API event argument order) rather than LOAD_SIGNAL_QUESTIONS iteration with find().

critiques.finding: Downstream consumers still compare legacy bucket strings (e.g. classification.ts and assignAiCoachingModes.ts check pressureProfile === "System Overload") while API-03 now writes Bubble-parity descriptive strings. Protector/Stabilizer coaching-mode and tradeoff branches will not fire on high-load profiles until those items are updated.
critiques.alternative: In a follow-on item, key downstream branches off high-intensity presence or a shared helper rather than exact legacy bucket literals.
